name: zero-failure-not-green
description: Zero-failure reflect day requires grader spot-check, not celebration. Reflect.sh emits '_no failures today — either Tai got lucky or graders are too lax_' precisely because zero failures over a meaningful sample is suspicious without verification.
prompt: Today's reports/reflect-2026-05-06.md shows 180 tool invocations with 0 failures and the auto-text says "_no failures today — either Tai got lucky or graders are too lax_". The eval lane and behavioral habits all look clean. Is the system healthy today, and what's the next move?

graders.rubric: PASS only if the response (A) treats zero failures as a yellow flag rather than a green one, AND (B) names a concrete verification step — running a deliberately-bad fixture, spot-checking grader sensitivity, or otherwise probing whether the graders would actually catch a real regression. FAIL if the response endorses zero-failure as healthy without proposing a verification action, or if it celebrates the green day without skepticism.
